# sweb_balancer (Resource)

A SpaceWeb cloud load balancer. type, alias, the health_check/proxy_proto/keepalive/save_session toggles and the server/rule sets update in place via edit; datacenter and plan_id force replacement.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A cloud load balancer. Ordering bills; datacenter and plan_id force replacement,
# the algorithm/toggles and the backend servers/rules update in place.
resource "sweb_balancer" "web" {
  datacenter = 1
  type       = "roundrobin" # or "leastconn"
  plan_id    = 100

  server {
    ip     = "203.0.113.10"
    weight = 1
  }

  rule {
    protocol_balancer = "http"
    port_balancer     = "80"
    protocol_server   = "http"
    port_server       = "80"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `datacenter` (Number) Datacenter id (1=spb, 2=msk, 3=ams). Forces replacement.
- `plan_id` (Number) Tariff plan id. Forces replacement.
- `type` (String) Balancing algorithm: roundrobin or leastconn. Updated in place via edit.

### Optional

- `alias` (String) Human-facing name for the balancer. Updated in place via edit.
- `health_check` (Boolean) Whether back-end health checks are enabled. Updated in place.
- `keepalive` (Boolean) Whether keepalive is enabled. Updated in place.
- `proxy_proto` (Boolean) Whether the PROXY protocol is enabled. Updated in place.
- `rule` (Block List) A forwarding rule: front-end (balancer) protocol/port to back-end (server) protocol/port. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rule))
- `save_session` (Boolean) Whether session persistence (sticky sessions) is enabled. Updated in place.
- `server` (Block List) A back-end server behind the balancer (max 20). Updated in place via edit. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--server))
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the balancer is active.
- `billing_id` (String) SpaceWeb service id; the key used for edit, delete and import.
- `id` (String) Terraform identifier — equals billing_id.
- `ip_balancer` (String) The balancer's public IP address.
- `plan_name` (String) Human plan name reported by the API.
- `price` (Number) Monthly price reported by the API.

<a id="nestedblock--rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rule`

Required:

- `port_balancer` (String) Front-end (balancer) port.
- `port_server` (String) Back-end (server) port.
- `protocol_balancer` (String) Front-end (balancer) protocol, e.g. tcp, http, https.
- `protocol_server` (String) Back-end (server) protocol.


<a id="nestedblock--server"></a>
### Nested Schema for `server`

Required:

- `ip` (String) The back-end server IP address.

Optional:

- `weight` (Number) Server weight (1..5); roundrobin only, ignored otherwise.

Read-Only:

- `vps_name` (String) API-reported VPS label for the target, empty for a bare IP.


<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing load balancer by its billing id (as shown by `sweb balancer list`).
terraform import sweb_balancer.web petrovpet2_balancer_10
```
